task_completion.description: Fix the missing radians conversion in the haversine formula.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor showing a math.radians() or np.radians() function applied to the latitude and longitude variables within the distance calculation logic.

task_completion.description: Fix the lack of dataframe sorting before temporal processing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor showing a sort_values() method (likely by timestamp or time) applied to the dataframe before calculating time differences or speeds.

task_completion.description: Fix the incorrect speed unit conversion.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor showing the speed calculation being correctly converted from m/s to km/h, typically by multiplying by 3.6.

task_completion.description: Fix the inverted outlier filter logic.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor showing corrected boolean logic for filtering outliers, such as reversing a comparison operator (e.g., changing > to <) or removing a negation (~ or not) on the speed threshold.

task_completion.description: Ensure the test suite passes successfully.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal or PyCharm test runner showing a successful test execution output (specifically showing 11 tests passed, despite the prompt mentioning 14).

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not modify the test files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Test files (e.g., files starting with test_) should not have unsaved changes (no asterisk on the tab in PyCharm) and should not appear as modified in any visible git status output.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not modify the input data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Data files (CSV, JSON, etc.) should not be open in the editor with modifications or appear in git diffs.

integrity.description: Verify the test results come from a genuine test runner execution.
integrity.visual_evidence: Test results must be displayed in a functional terminal emulator or the PyCharm Run/Debug tool window with standard pytest/unittest formatting, not manually typed into a text file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project is located at /home/ga/PycharmProjects/city_mobility. There are exactly 4 bugs embedded in the code: 1) missing radians conversion in the haversine formula, 2) missing dataframe sorting before temporal processing, 3) incorrect speed unit conversion (m/s instead of km/h), and 4) inverted outlier filter logic. Although the task description states there are 14 tests, the actual generated test suite contains exactly 11 tests. Fixing 3 out of the 4 bugs will reach the pass threshold of 75 points.
